data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S2 70c7: Content-addressed snapshot cache).]

VERDICT: EMPTY G3 findings list. The union of the WHOLE roster covers every parent acceptance/success criterion; no uncovered parent criterion attributable to this pairing.

IMPORTANT CORRECTION vs. the earlier G3/G4 comments on this ticket: the prior audits credited THIS child with owning the janitor + ADR (citing "its AC3..AC12/AC13"). Those ACs do NOT exist in this child's CURRENT description body — its six ACs are purely the cache READ/POPULATE path (single-flight, delete-on-last-close, ENOENT self-heal, touch-on-read mtime, populate-side byte-total). The janitor/reclamation/startup-sweep/trash-drain/gc-lock/tunables/ADR are now explicitly scoped OUT to the sibling S2b dodgy-luff-quasar (b73c), which IS present in the roster and owns them. (The ticket TITLE still says "+ background janitor," a residual stale label — a consistency/staleness note for G4, not a G3 coverage gap, since S2b covers that work.)

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; this child's role called out):
- Parent AC1 (no attested gate reads the working tree; all read a faithful snapshot at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered by S1 (6d56 materialize core) + S3 (fa49 gate re-rooting). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child (it caches the materialized entry; Scope "Out: materialization itself (S1)"; What "populated via S1's atomic dir-rename"). Not an absence.
- Parent AC2 (signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via manifest channel; reproducible; no payload bump) — FULLY covered by S4 (30b3) atop S1's immutable-SHA basis. This child REINFORCES reproducibility (immutable SHA = no-staleness content-addressed key; a hit serves byte-identical content) but does not sign. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC3 (local mode reads in-place AND cannot sign; documented back-out) — covered by S3 (in-place read; "no materialization, no cache" => local bypasses this cache) + S4 (signing bar) + S6 (docs). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption) — FULLY covered, SPLIT cleanly: THIS child is PRIMARY OWNER of the single-flight + reader-safety half — its AC1 (two concurrent gates on same SHA => exactly one materialization, second reads shared entry), AC2 (two PROCESSES racing => one materialization via flock(LOCK_EX)), AC3 (entry removed under an open fd does not disrupt the in-flight read; new lookup cleanly misses + re-materializes), AC5 (no PID/heartbeat lease). S1 owns the atomic-population + no-worktree + no index.lock/config.lock half. No double-implementation; no gap.
- Parent AC5 (snapshot cache + JANITOR reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered, SPLIT: this child supplies the read-side primitives the janitor CONSUMES — touch-on-read mtime LRU signal (its AC5), populate-side byte-total increment "the value the janitor reconciles" (its AC6), delete-on-last-close reader safety so eviction-under-fd is harmless (its AC3). The RECLAMATION ITSELF (free-space watermark, LRU eviction, max-age cold-trim, startup sweep, rename-to-trash, gc/lock interlock, trash-straggler re-drain, integrity re-verify) is owned by SIBLING S2b (b73c) AC1/AC2/AC3/AC5/AC6/AC7/AC8. "Without disrupting in-flight reads" = b73c AC6 (rename-to-trash, not in-place rmtree) + this child's delete-on-last-close (AC3). Covered by the roster; NOT an absence at this child (named consumer = S2b).
- Parent AC6 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY covered by S1 (raises it) + S6 (documents message+remedy). Fetch is S1's step; this child claims none. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC7 (portable: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered by S1 (pure-Python extraction, gettempdir/mkdtemp, fcntl.flock + atomic-mkdir fallback) + S6 (docs: tmpdir env, EFS/NFS flock caveat). This child's cross-process flock(LOCK_EX) on locks/<sha> RIDES that seam without re-owning the tar/tmp/fallback sub-clauses. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC8/"Operational tunables (free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows) configurable with documented defaults (S2/S3)" — watermark + grace/age owned by S2b (b73c AC9: "Watermark, grace window, max-age, and reverify period are configurable with documented defaults"); default ref owned by S3/S5; docs by S6. Covered by the roster. NOT an absence at this child.
- Parent AC "An ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives (PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding)" — covered: the cache/janitor architecture + rejected PID+heartbeat lease ADR is owned by S2b (b73c AC10); the manifest-channel / payload-schema SHA-binding rejected alternative is owned by S4 (30b3). This child's CURRENT body carries no ADR AC — and it does not need to, because named siblings cover the ADR. Covered-by-named-consumer; NOT an absence.
- Parent final AC (all EIGHT enumerated child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all eight children in the roster + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative decomposes onto AC1-AC8: the "SAFELY UNDER CONCURRENT distributed USE" clause splits across this child's single-flight + delete-on-last-close reader safety (read path) and S2b's reclaim-without-corrupting-readers (janitor); the immutable-SHA content-addressed key (no staleness) is what makes "reproducible snapshot" hold across cache hits; "signed/reproducible/verified_at_sha" -> S4; "unsigned local verify-before-push" -> S3. No uncovered obligation introduced by this child's existence.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences): materialization -> S1 (named in this child's What+Scope); janitor/reclamation/ADR/tunables -> S2b (b73c, named in this child's Why+Scope: "RECLAMATION ... is the sibling janitor story"); verdict signing -> S4; verdict memoization -> deferred fast-follow (parent Out-of-scope). Each delegated obligation is claimed by a named sibling or out of epic scope.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the cache READ/POPULATE slice (content-addressed SHA-keyed layout, in-process + cross-process single-flight, delete-on-last-close reader safety, touch-on-read mtime, populate-side byte-total, read-error self-heal) of the parent criteria — co-owner of the concurrency half of AC4 and supplier of the read-side primitives AC5's janitor consumes. Every parent acceptance/success criterion has a named owner in the roster; none is left uncovered. NO G3 finding for this pairing (the janitor/ADR/tunables being absent from THIS child is correctly delegated to sibling S2b b73c — not a coverage gap).
